notes: Use for exploration only, not final pixel masters.
Prioritize readable silhouettes and costume blocks over texture detail.

recommended: Pixel-native Illustrious or SDXL sprite checkpoint
notes: This is the main family to benchmark first.
Reject any checkpoint that cannot keep clean clusters after downscale.
Do not replace this family with DreamShaper for final production.

ui_or_tiles_specialist.role: optional second pixel checkpoint for ui modules and tile kits
recommended: Second pixel-native checkpoint tuned for icons, tiles, or low-noise sprites
notes: Only keep this lane if it materially beats the main pixel master on tileability or anti-text behavior.
If it does not clearly win, delete it and keep one pixel master family.

rules: Do not default to global LoRA stacks.
Use at most one verified lane-specific LoRA in the first benchmark pass.
Avoid grain, detail, painterly, and cinematic LoRAs in final pixel passes.
targets: optional SNES or JRPG palette bias LoRA

forbidden: global detail enhancers in final export
